page_content: The University of Maryland, College Park campus, afﬁrms the right of the faculty to determine course content and curriculum requirements. The University, however, also encourages faculty to consider offering alternatives to the use of animals in their courses. In each course, the instructor determines whether the use of animals in the classroom exercises will be a course requirement or optional activity. The following departments currently have courses that may require animals to be used in class activities: Animal Sciences, Human Nutrition and Food Systems, Microbiology, Poultry Science, Psychology, Veterinary Medicine, and Zoology.
